item.title: Neurocrine Biosciences / Soleno Therapeutics Acquisition (Vykat)
item.description:
• Summary: Neurocrine Biosciences has agreed to acquire Soleno Therapeutics for approximately $2.9 billion, marking its largest M&A deal to date. The acquisition centers on Vykat (diazoxide choline), Soleno's lead asset for Prader-Willi syndrome, a rare genetic disorder characterized by chronic hunger and obesity. Vykat, an oral extended-release tablet, has shown positive late-stage data, and Neurocrine aims to leverage its commercial infrastructure to launch the potential blockbuster. The deal represents a strategic expansion into rare diseases for Neurocrine, which is known for its neurology and endocrine portfolio.
  • MoAs: Vykat (diazoxide choline): Potassium channel activator, believed to modulate neuronal activity and reduce hyperphagia.
  • Sources: BioPharma Dive, Fierce Pharma, BioSpace, STAT News, Endpoints News

item.title: Takeda / Denali Partnership Termination
item.description:
• Summary: Takeda has terminated its partnership with Denali Therapeutics, returning rights to an undisclosed dementia asset. The move is part of Takeda's ongoing restructuring efforts to refocus its R&D portfolio. The partnership, initiated to develop Denali's transport vehicle technology for neurodegenerative diseases, is being dissolved as Takeda prioritizes other therapeutic areas. This decision underscores the strategic shifts within large pharma regarding high-risk, long-term neuroscience investments.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Fierce Biotech, Endpoints News, BioSpace

item.title: Anthropic / Coefficient Bio Acquisition
item.description:
• Summary: AI giant Anthropic has acquired stealth-mode biotechnology startup Coefficient Bio for $400 million, signaling a significant move into the life sciences sector. The acquisition aims to combine Anthropic's advanced AI capabilities with Coefficient's drug discovery platform. While financial terms were disclosed, the deal highlights the growing convergence of artificial intelligence and biopharmaceutical R&D as companies seek to accelerate therapeutic development.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Fierce Biotech, Endpoints News, BioSpace

item.title: Syneron Funding
item.description:
• Summary: China-based biotech Syneron has raised $150 million in a new funding round to advance its peptide-based therapeutic platform. This investment adds to the $100 million raised last year, underscoring strong investor interest in novel peptide modalities for various diseases. The capital will support R&D and pipeline expansion.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: Endpoints News

item.title: UnitedHealth Group AI Push
item.description:
• Summary: UnitedHealth Group is aggressively investing in artificial intelligence to transform healthcare delivery, with initiatives focused on diagnostics, operational efficiency, and personalized patient care. This corporate push aims to leverage AI for predictive analytics and cost reduction, potentially reshaping healthcare management and outcomes.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: STAT News, STAT News

item.title: Endpoints News Dealmaking Roundup
item.description:
• Summary: The pharma industry is experiencing a surge in dealmaking activity, with Blackstone raising a record fund for healthcare investments. Xolair (omalizumab) is energizing allergy research, indicating renewed interest in immunology therapeutics. This roundup covers mergers, acquisitions, and partnerships shaping sector dynamics.
  • MoAs: Xolair is an anti-immunoglobulin E (IgE) monoclonal antibody that inhibits IgE-mediated allergic responses by binding to free IgE and downregulating IgE receptors.
  • Sources: Endpoints News
